title: France Lowers 2030 Hydrogen Targets Amid Market Challenges, Maintains â‚¬4Bn Subsidy Commitment
content: France has revised its national hydrogen strategy, lowering its 2030 hydrogen electrolysis capacity target from 6.5GW to 4.5GW due to slower market and technological progress. The 2035 target has also been reduced from 10GW to 8GW. Despite these adjustments, the French government remains committed to providing â‚¬4 billion ($4.5 billion USD) in subsidies through 2040 to support low-carbon hydrogen production. The strategy emphasizes that hydrogen remains a key part of Franceâ€™s decarbonization efforts, with continued focus on commercialization, infrastructure, and research.
author: Analysts Account
